meaning: vt.使凋谢,使消亡,使畏缩vi.枯萎,衰退,感到羞愧
examples: In autumn the leaves wither and fall off the trees.
During months without water under a harsh sun, they wither, shrink and contract until they look like a pile of dead gray leaves.
The flower went on, "My days have passed and I'm going to wither. That makes me so sad."
Otherwise they will wither in a matter of months once emotional closeness is lost, Dunbar says.
They are recognising the importance of literacy and we are allowing ours to wither.
